mfa.note: distinctive pyruvate overflow instead of acetate

growth_records.cite: Tannler, Simon et al. Maintenance metabolism and carbon fluxes in Bacillus species. Microbial Cell Factories (2008). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-7-19. [PMC2442585]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2: B. amyloliquefaciens Growth rate 0.32; Yield 0.23; Glucose consumption 6.05; Pyruvate production 5.45 (acetate n/a). its overflow metabolism is entirely different, secreting massive amounts of pyruvate instead of acetate.
growth_records.notes: B. amyloliquefaciens glucose batch 37C pH7.4 aerobic (250 mL bioreactor, 2 vvm). mu=0.32/h; td=ln2/0.32=2.17h. qGlc=6.05, qPyruvate=5.45 mmol/gCDW/h (no acetate); Yxs=0.23 (lowest). Chemostat maintenance m_glc=0.35 +/- 0.13 mmol/gCDW/h (Table 3).
